item.text: I’ve confirmed the two hotspots: exit-code routing in `result-contracts.ts` and continuation-handle validation in `story-implementor.ts`. Next I’m reading the command/test surfaces so we can patch the behavior and lock it down with CLI regressions.

I’ve got the shape now: both commands already rely on the shared envelope status, so the exit fix belongs centrally. I’m checking the result-envelope helper and current continuation tests before patching, so we keep the behavior change tight and avoid disturbing unrelated command flows.

I found an important nuance: the Copilot adapter already labels retained-session resumes as `CONTINUATION_HANDLE_INVALID`, but the workflow is still allowed to reach adapter dispatch first. I’m moving that rejection earlier so `story-continue` fails for the right reason before any provider execution path is touched, then I’ll add the CLI regressions for both fixes.

item.aggregated_output:
node bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs epic-cleanup --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --cleanup-batch /abs/spec-pack/artifacts/cleanup/cleanup-batch.md --json
```

For commands whose inner payload also contains an `outcome`, the envelope `outcome` is the routing source of truth and must match the inner payload exactly. Schema validation should reject mismatches.

### Exit Codes

Exit codes are a coarse routing signal. The JSON payload remains the source of truth.

| Exit Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `0` | Command completed and produced a non-blocking outcome (`ready`, `ready-for-verification`, `pass`, `cleaned`, `ready-for-closeout`) |
| `2` | Command completed but requires follow-up (`needs-user-decision`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `revise`, `needs-more-routing`, `needs-more-cleanup`, `needs-fixes`, `needs-more-verification`) |
| `3` | Command is blocked by environment, spec-pack, config, or provider readiness issue |
| `1` | Invalid invocation, schema parse failure, or uncaught runtime error |

### Result Routing Matrix

This matrix is authoritative for handler implementation and tests.

| Command Outcome | Envelope Status | Exit Code | Orchestrator Action |
|---|---|---:|---|
| `ready` | `ok` | 0 | proceed to next setup step |
| `ready-for-verification` | `ok` | 0 | run verification or next bounded step |
| `pass` | `ok` | 0 | evaluate for acceptance or next stage |
| `cleaned` | `ok` | 0 | move into epic verification |
| `ready-for-closeout` | `ok` | 0 | run final orchestrator-owned gate |
| `needs-user-decision` | `needs-user-decision` | 2 | pause and request human clarification |
| `needs-followup-fix` | `ok` | 2 | route same-session follow-up or fix path |
| `needs-human-ruling` | `needs-user-decision` | 2 | pause for orchestrator/human ruling |
| `revise` | `ok` | 2 | route fix and rerun verification |
| `needs-more-routing` | `ok` | 2 | select another bounded correction path |
| `needs-more-cleanup` | `ok` | 2 | continue cleanup cycle |
| `needs-fixes` | `ok` | 2 | route fixes before closeout |
| `needs-more-verification` | `ok` | 2 | launch additional verification/synthesis |
| `blocked` | `blocked` | 3 | stop and inspect blocking error details |
| parse/schema/runtime failure | `error` | 1 | treat as CLI failure rather than feature-state outcome |

## Runtime Module Layout

```text
processes/impl-cli/
├── cli.ts
├── commands/
│   ├── inspect.ts
│   ├── preflight.ts
│   ├── story-implement.ts
│   ├── story-continue.ts
│   ├── story-verify.ts
│   ├── quick-fix.ts
│   ├── epic-cleanup.ts

item.text: I’ve got the routing matrix in hand, and it confirms the exact split we need: some `ok` outcomes are still follow-up exits. I’m patching the shared mapper now, then I’ll add one CLI regression each for `story-implement` and `story-continue`, plus the upfront Copilot-handle rejection test.

item.text: I’m at the edit point now. The code change will be small but touches the shared routing helper plus every CLI command that consumes it, so I’m updating those together and then wiring the focused regressions around the public command surface.

The implementation patch is in. I’m adding the three regressions now: two exit-code checks through the public commands, and one forged Copilot continuation-handle case that proves we now reject before any provider dispatch path.
The regressions are in place. I’m running the focused `impl-cli` tests first to catch any typing or contract issues before we spend time on the full baseline capture and green verify.

item.text:
Files changed: `processes/impl-cli/core/result-contracts.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/core/story-implementor.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/commands/story-implement.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/commands/story-continue.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/commands/inspect.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/commands/preflight.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/tests/story-implement-command.test.ts`, and `processes/impl-cli/tests/story-continue-command.test.ts`.

Tests added: a `story-implement` CLI regression for `needs-followup-fix -> exit 2`, a matching `story-continue` CLI regression, and a `story-continue` regression that rejects forged Copilot continuation handles with `CONTINUATION_HANDLE_INVALID` instead of `PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE`.

Gate result: `bun run capture:test-baseline --force` passed, and `bun run green-verify` passed cleanly (`160 pass, 0 fail`).
